title: YouTube subscriptions are now generating around $20bn a year - Music Ally
text:
Tech giant Alphabet’s
latest financial results are out
, and they reveal how much YouTube made from advertising in the final quarter of 2025, as well as the year as a whole.
YouTube ads generated $11.38bn of revenue in Q4 last year, taking the total for 2025 to $40.37bn. That was up 11.7% year-on-year: an impressive performance given the wobbles in the wider advertising economy.
The results don’t specifically break out figures for YouTube’s subscriptions business, but CEO Sundar Pichai
told analysts
that “we continue to see strong subscription revenue growth across YouTube – particularly YouTube Music Premium”.
But wait. In the same earnings call, SVP and chief business officer Philipp Schindler said that “for the full year 2025, our YouTube’s annual revenue surpassed $60 billion across ads and subscription”.
That suggests that subscriptions are now a roughly-$20bn annual business for YouTube. Music is key to that, and continues to be central to the platform’s culture and viewing activity. Witness Omdia’s recent estimate that
music is now a third of all YouTube watch-time
.
YouTube said last March that it had
125 million subscribers to its YouTube Music and YouTube Premium tiers
– the latter of which includes YouTube Music. The figure included people on free trials.
The company also revealed last October that its annual payouts to the music industry are
now more than $8bn
. Expect all these figures to drive the conversations next time YouTube and rightsholders are renewing licensing deals.
Stuart Dredge
Music Ally's Head of Insight
More by Stuart Dredge
